perturbation.original_text: References to "Confidential Information" mean all knowledge, information or materials whether of a technical or financial nature or otherwise relating to the business or affairs of the Parties (including without limitation any subsidiary or affiliated entity thereof), including all memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other materials prepared by or for the receiving party which contain or reflect such knowledge, information or materials, which is provided or disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity and identified at the time of such disclosure as being confidential; provided that Confidential Information shall not include: (a) information which the receiving party can demonstrate was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure by the disclosing party; (b) information which is in the public domain or which enters the public domain other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement; or (c) information that the receiving party independently develops or independently becomes aware of from a third party without, to the knowledge of the receiving party, a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: References to "Protected Information" mean all knowledge, information or materials whether of a technical or financial nature or otherwise relating to the business or affairs of the Parties (including without limitation any subsidiary or affiliated entity thereof), including all memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other materials prepared by or for the receiving party which contain or reflect such knowledge, information or materials, which is provided or disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity and identified at the time of such disclosure as being confidential; provided that Protected Information shall not include: (a) information which the receiving party can demonstrate was known to the receiving party prior to disclosure by the disclosing party; (b) information which is in the public domain or which enters the public domain other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement; or (c) information that the receiving party independently develops or independently becomes aware of from a third party without, to the knowledge of the receiving party, a duty of confidentiality to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is a key defined term. Changing it to 'Protected Information' without consistent replacement throughout the document creates ambiguity. Specifically, Section 2 still refers to 'Confidential Information' creating a contradiction, since its definition now belongs to a new term, 'Protected Information'. It is therefore unclear which type of Information, the rest of the agreement is referring to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information'. The changed text redefines that term as 'Protected Information' but the contradicted text still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction because the original definition is now misaligned with the continued use of 'Confidential Information' in other parts of the document. This is a strong contradiction and adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Confidential Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Private Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.explanation: In Section 2, the term 'Confidential Information' is replaced by 'Private Information' at the start of the section but kept as 'Confidential Information' later in the same section. This creates an internal contradiction within the same paragraph as to what type of information the receiving party must maintain the confidentiality of and what information should be disclosed legally. This creates uncertanty in the obligations of the receiving party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party agrees that it will at all times maintain the confidentiality of any Private Information communicated to it by or on behalf of the disclosing party; provided that in the event the receiving party becomes legally compelled (by deposition, interrogatory, requests for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Page 1 ​ of 4 Information, or the receiving party determines that it is obligated by statute or governmental regulation to disclose any of the Confidential Information, the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT written notice of such requirement so that the disclosing party, if possible, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the receiving party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is advised by counsel is legally required to be furnished and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' at the beginning of the section but retains 'Confidential Information' later in the same section. This creates a direct contradiction within the same paragraph regarding the type of information the receiving party must maintain confidentiality for and what information should be disclosed legally. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case) clearly shows the conflicting use of 'Private Information' and 'Confidential Information', creating uncertainty and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate (or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), nor shall it use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate (or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), nor shall it use the Confidential Data for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3, the term 'Confidential Information' is used, but is then replaced with 'Confidential Data' without defining 'Confidential Data.' This leads to uncertainty as to whether 'Data' and 'Information' are interchangeable in the context of this agreement, or if different standards apply for their treatment and use. The uncertainty is created because they are being used in the same sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise permitted by this Agreement, the receiving party agrees that it shall not copy, adapt, divulge, publish, disclose, or circulate (or authorize or permit anyone else to adapt, divulge, publish, disclose or circulate) any of the Confidential Information disclosed or communicated to it by the disclosing party (except as described in paragraph 4), nor shall it use the Confidential Data for any purpose other than the Opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in Section 3, while the contradicted text continues to use both terms, potentially creating uncertainty and contradiction. This creates a misalignment of terminology because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its employees, directors, officers, advisors, attorneys, auditors and agents only on a need-to-know basis in connection with the Opportunity, and each employee, director, officer, advisor, attorney, auditor or agent to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall treat such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The receiving party shall be liable for all acts and omissions of each person and entity to which the receiving party discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its employees, directors, officers, advisors, attorneys, auditors and agents only on a need-to-know basis in connection with the Opportunity, and each employee, director, officer, advisor, attorney, auditor or agent to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall treat such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The receiving party shall be liable for all acts and omissions of each person and entity to which the receiving party discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' at the start of Section 4, while maintaining 'Confidential Information' later in the same section, creates a contradiction. This makes it unclear which type of information the employees, directors, officers etc. should treat with consistency and for whose actions the receiving party is liable. The paragraph references both `Proprietary Information` and `Confidential Information` creating doubt about which is being regulated and handled and how.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its employees, directors, officers, advisors, attorneys, auditors and agents only on a need-to-know basis in connection with the Opportunity, and each employee, director, officer, advisor, attorney, auditor or agent to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall treat such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The receiving party shall be liable for all acts and omissions of each person and entity to which the receiving party discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text starts with 'Proprietary Information' but continues to refer to 'Confidential Information' within the same section. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and, as such, suffers from the same problem. This creates a direct in-text contradiction, making it unclear which type of information is being regulated and for whose actions the receiving party is liable. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', as the contract uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than set out herein, is granted to the receiving party hereunder by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Sensitive Data shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than set out herein, is granted to the receiving party hereunder by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 6, changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' mid-sentence introduces ambiguity. It becomes unclear whether the 'Sensitive Data' being held in trust refers to the same 'Confidential Information' mentioned at the beginning of the sentence, leading to confusion about what exactly is being protected and whose property it is. Since both terms are not the same, its uncertain what are the requirements in handling each.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Sensitive Data shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than set out herein, is granted to the receiving party hereunder by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' mid-sentence introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction. While the original text consistently refers to 'Confidential Information,' the changed text mixes the two terms, leading to uncertainty about whether the 'Sensitive Data' being held in trust refers to the same 'Confidential Information' mentioned at the beginning of the sentence. This contradicts the expectation of consistent terminology and obligations, as the requirements for handling each term may differ, making it unclear what is protected and whose property it is. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party hereby agrees that all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party hereby agrees that all Company Secrets (including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Company Secrets' within Section 7 introduces contradiction. It is unclear whether the restriction on copying and storage applies to 'Company Secrets', or just to 'Confidential Information'. This is because the beginning of the section talks about Confidential Information, while the later half refers to Company Secrets, which creates different requirements. If it's not copied and stored, is this term included?
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party hereby agrees that all Company Secrets (including all copies thereof) disclosed to or created by the receiving party shall be delivered forthwith by the receiving party to the disclosing party, or, at the election of the receiving party, destroyed, and deleted from all retrieval systems and data bases upon the earlier of a request at any time by the disclosing party or at any time the receiving party decides not to proceed with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Company Secrets' where 'Confidential Information' was previously used. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, uses both terms within the same section, creating a contradiction because the section begins by referencing 'Confidential Information' but then switches to 'Company Secrets'. This raises a question of whether the initial clause regarding copying and storage requirements applies to 'Company Secrets' as well, or just to 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition as the terms are used interchangeably without definition, leading to conflicting obligations within the section, and a strong in-text contradiction.
